pokemon:zorua.name: Zorua
pokemon:zorua.summary: To protect themselves from danger, they hide their true identities by transforming into people and Pokémon.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: To protect themselves from danger, they hide their true identities by transforming into people and Pokémon.
It changes into the forms of others to surprise them. Apparently, it often transforms into a silent child.
It changes so it looks just like its foe, tricks it, and then uses that opportunity to flee.
A once-departed soul, returned to life in Hisui. Derives power from resentment, which rises as energy atop its head and takes on the forms of foes. In this way, Zorua vents lingering malice.
It changes so it looks just like its foe, tricks it, and then uses that opportunity to flee.
Zorua sometimes transforms into a person and goes into cities to search for food. When Zorua does this, it usually takes on the form of a child.
Zorua is a timid Pokémon. This disposition seems to be what led to the development of Zorua’s ability to take on the forms of other creatures.
If a normally talkative child suddenly stops talking, it may have been replaced by Zorua.
This Pokémon has a cowardly disposition, so when it’s not around friends, it basically always stays transformed as something else.
To protect themselves from danger, they hide their true identities by transforming into people and Pokémon.
It changes so it looks just like its foe, tricks it, and then uses that opportunity to flee.
It changes so it looks just like its foe, tricks it, and then uses that opportunity to flee.
To protect themselves from danger, they hide their true identities by transforming into people and Pokémon.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: To protect themselves from danger, they hide their true identities by transforming into people and Pokémon.

pokemon:zoroark.name: Zoroark
pokemon:zoroark.summary: Bonds between these Pokémon are very strong. It protects the safety of its pack by tricking its opponents.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Bonds between these Pokémon are very strong. It protects the safety of its pack by tricking its opponents.
Bonds between these Pokémon are very strong. It protects the safety of its pack by tricking its opponents.
Stories say those who tried to catch Zoroark were trapped in an illusion and punished.
With its disheveled white fur, it looks like an embodiment of death. Heedless of its own safety, Zoroark attacks its nemeses with a bitter energy so intense, it lacerates Zoroark's own body.
Each has the ability to fool a large group of people simultaneously. They protect their lair with illusory scenery.
Seeking to ease the burden of their solitude, lonely Trainers tell Zoroark to show illusions to them.
This Pokémon cares deeply about others of its kind, and it will conjure terrifying illusions to keep its den and pack safe.
If it thinks humans are going to discover its den, Zoroark shows them visions that make them wander around in the woods.
It doesn’t just transform itself—it also has the power to make hundreds of people see its illusions.
Each has the ability to fool a large group of people simultaneously. They protect their lair with illusory scenery.
Stories say those who tried to catch Zoroark were trapped in an illusion and punished.
Each has the ability to fool a large group of people simultaneously. They protect their lair with illusory scenery.
Bonds between these Pokémon are very strong. It protects the safety of its pack by tricking its opponents.

loreSummary: Bonds between these Pokémon are very strong. It protects the safety of its pack by tricking its opponents.
